title: Father stabs son and is arrested after finding bodies of wife and daughter in Nagano
summary: A 46-year-old man was arrested for attempted murder of his son, who alerted the police; his ex-wife and daughter were found dead in the home in Tōmi city.

title: Torrential rains and linear precipitation zones cause massive evacuations in Kyushu
summary: The prefectures of Saga, Nagasaki, Oita, Fukuoka, and Kumamoto were affected by heavy rains that triggered landslide and flood warnings, with evacuation orders for hundreds of thousands of people.

title: Fukuoka Kōtarō, 20, proclaimed new Honinbo of Go
summary: The young talent defeated Ichiriki Ryo in the fifth game of the tournament, taking away the title and breaking into the elite of professional Japanese Go.

title: 10-year-old boy who disappeared during class found dead at a waterfall
summary: The minor, a student at a special education school, left the classroom to go to the bathroom and was found dead 1.5 kilometers away; the school principal offered a public apology for the incident.

title: Imō Fujitarō, living national treasure of shamisen, dies at 90
summary: The nagauta musician and composer, recognized for his mastery of the shamisen and his work in both classical and contemporary pieces, passed away leaving a cultural legacy in Japan.

title: Japanese Prime Minister Takaichi begins official visit to India to strengthen bilateral cooperation
summary: On her first trip to India, Takaichi signed agreements on economic security and artificial intelligence and promoted a Japanese investment of 10 trillion yen in the country.

title: Earthquake of magnitude 6.0 shakes Aomori and Iwate prefectures in Japan
summary: The quake, with epicenter off the coast of Iwate, reached a shindo of 4 on the Japanese scale and did not generate a tsunami warning.

title: Sony plans to stop distributing PlayStation games on disc starting in 2028
summary: The Japanese company announced that new titles for its console will only be available in digital format, putting an end to the sale of physical PlayStation discs.

title: Kato Takayuki throws a complete game shutout and ties for the lead in wins in Japanese baseball league
summary: The pitcher for the Nippon Ham Fighters defeated the Orix Buffaloes with a complete game shutout, achieving his eighth win of the season.

title: Fujii Souta achieves his seventh consecutive Kisei title in shogi
summary: The shogi prodigy defeated Hattori Shinichiro 3-0 in the final and extends his streak of consecutive wins to 14 official games.

title: Supervisor of gas leak at Mitsui Chemicals plant in Ōmuta referred to prosecutors
summary: For the accident at the factory, the supervisor was charged with professional negligence resulting in injuries to workers and neighbors.

title: Japanese Foreign Minister Motegi promises support to Ukraine in meeting with counterpart
summary: Motegi met in Kyiv with the Ukrainian foreign minister and announced assistance to restore world heritage sites and cooperate on security matters.

title: SoftBank Hawks offer number 1 jersey to young prospect Sasaki Rintarou
summary: The standout college baseball player, who is considering offers from NPB and Major League Baseball, expressed gratitude for the offer from the Japanese professional team.

title: Japan Football Association asks Moriyasu to continue as national team coach
summary: He would be offered a one-year contract to lead the Japanese national team, in an effort to maintain team stability for upcoming tournaments.

title: Donald Trump reports over 230 billion yen in cryptocurrency profits
summary: According to a 2025 asset report, the former US president's crypto-friendly policies would have generated enormous profits for him.

title: Number of hibakusha in Japan decreases to 91,000 and their average age exceeds 86 years
summary: The Ministry of Health reported that atomic bomb survivors are continuing to decrease, with an average age of 86.66 years.

title: Tokyo Police warns schoolchildren about the risks of illegal jobs on the dark web
summary: Officers from the Tokyo Metropolitan Police visited junior high schools to alert young people about criminal networks that recruit for illegal activities over the internet.

title: Mount Fuji climbing season opens with access controls and fees
summary: From July 1, climbers can ascend Japan's most iconic mountain, which this year implements a payment system and visitor limit to prevent overcrowding.

title: Tension in the Taiwan Strait: Xi Jinping insists on unification while Lai Ching-te defends difference
summary: In a speech for the 105th anniversary of the Communist Party, Xi reiterated that unification is a historical task, while the Taiwanese president emphasized that Taiwan is fundamentally different from China and will resist unification pressure.

title: Yen weakens to 162 yen per dollar and drags down retail stocks
summary: The Japanese currency hit lows of 162.70 yen against the dollar, amid expectations of a rate hike in the US, impacting retail companies and creating inflationary pressures.
